items.headline: Special Weather Statement issued April 23 at 5:14AM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
A series of frontal systems will lift a plume of moisture from the
tropical Pacific into Prince William Sound and along the coastal
mountains through Friday. The bulk of this precipitation will
fall in the form of heavy wet snow through Thompson Pass as
temperatures hover between 29 and 33 degrees. 8 to 16 inches of
snow is possible through Saturday morning, with the heaviest snow
likely Thursday morning through Thursday afternoon and again early
Friday morning through Friday afternoon. Southeas

items.headline: High Wind Warning issued April 23 at 4:22AM AKDT until April 24 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...South winds 40 to 50 mph with gusts up to 75 mph.

* WHERE...Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.
items.areas: Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp
items.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

items.headline: Wind Advisory issued April 23 at 4:22AM AKDT until April 24 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...South winds 35 to 45 mph with gusts up to 65 mph expected.

* WHERE...Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.
items.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 23 at 4:07AM AKDT until April 23 at 12:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow and blowing snow. Additional snow accumulations up to
one inch. Winds gusting as high as 35 mph.

* WHERE...Point Lay.

* WHEN...Until noon AKDT today.

* IMPACTS...Travel may be impacted. Areas of blowing snow could
reduce visibility.
items.instruction: Slow down and use caution while traveling.

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 23 at 4:00AM AKDT until April 23 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow. Additional snow accumulations up to two inches.

* WHERE...Interior Seward Peninsula and Southern Seward Peninsula
Coast.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT this afternoon.

* IMPACTS...Travel may be impacted due to areas of blowing snow.
items.areas: Southern Seward Peninsula Coast; Interior Seward Peninsula
items.instruction:
Slow down and use caution while traveling. Visit 511.alaska.gov or
call 5 1 1 for the latest road conditions.

items.headline: Winter Weather Advisory issued April 23 at 4:00AM AKDT until April 23 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Snow. Additional snow accumulations up to one inch.

* WHERE...Baldwin Peninsula, Lower Kobuk Valley, and Northern Seward
Peninsula.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT this afternoon.

* IMPACTS...Travel may be impacted due to areas of blowing snow.
items.areas: Lower Kobuk Valley; Baldwin Peninsula; Northern Seward Peninsula
items.instruction: Slow down and use caution while traveling.

items.headline: Blizzard Warning issued April 23 at 4:00AM AKDT until April 23 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Blizzard conditions. Additional snow accumulations up to
one inch.

* WHERE...Bering Strait Coast.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT this afternoon.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be difficult. Areas of blowing snow could
significantly reduce visibility.
items.instruction:
Travel should be restricted to emergencies only. If you must travel,
have a winter survival kit with you. If you get stranded, stay with
your vehicle. Visit 511.alaska.gov or call 5 1 1 for the latest road
conditions.

items.headline: Blizzard Warning issued April 23 at 4:00AM AKDT until April 24 at 4:00AM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Blizzard conditions. Additional snow accumulations between
2 and 4 inches. Winds gusting as high as 45 mph.

* WHERE...St Lawrence Island.

* WHEN...Until 4 AM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be very difficult. Areas of blowing snow
could significantly reduce visibility. Strong winds could result
in power outages.
items.instruction:
Travel should be restricted to emergencies only. If you must travel,
have a winter survival kit with you. If you get stranded, stay with
your vehicle.

items.headline: Flood Watch issued April 22 at 12:36PM AKDT until April 25 at 11:00AM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by rain and snowmelt is possible.

* WHERE...The following areas, Central Susitna Valley, Southern
Susitna Valley and Western Susitna Valley.  This includes the
communities of Skwentna, Willow, Talkeetna, Trapper Creek, And
Petersville.

* WHEN...From 9 PM AKDT this evening through Saturday morning.

* IMPACTS...Flooding of creeks, streams, low-lying and flood-prone
locations will be possible as a prolonged period of rainfall is
expected Wednesday night into Saturday mor
items.areas: Western Susitna Valley; Southern Susitna Valley; Central Susitna Valley
items.instruction:
Persons living along streams should stay alert and be prepared to
take action if a warning or advisory is issued, or flooding is
observed.

You should monitor later forecasts and be alert for possible Flood
